name: Dartmouth College
summary: Dartmouth College is the site of the 1956 Dartmouth Conference, the founding event of artificial intelligence as a formal academic discipline. John McCarthy, then an assistant professor of mathematics at Dartmouth, organized the conference along with Marvin Minsky (Harvard), Nathaniel Rochester (IBM), and Claude Shannon (Bell Labs). The conference proposal coined the term 'artificial intelligence' and brought together the researchers who would define the field for decades. While Dartmouth's ongoing AI research has been modest compared to MIT, Stanford, or Carnegie Mellon, its place in AI history is permanently secured as the birthplace of the field.
